name: Burosque Enchanted Forest and Pozuelo de Aragón Geyser Loop
name_original: Burosque Encantado de Bureta y Géiser de Pozuelo de Aragón — circular desde Borja
description: Cycle 22.9 miles through the Campo de Borja region to discover the whimsical Enchanted Forest of Bureta and the Pozuelo de Aragon Geyser.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Borja offers routes through a landscape characterized by extensive pine forests and mountainous terrain. The region features abundant coniferous and deciduous forests covering its slopes, with the highest peak, Velika Runjevica, reaching 1077 meters. These natural features provide varied cycling routes and scenic trails. The area's elevation changes contribute to diverse cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Borja The most popular touring cycling route is Burosque Enchanted Forest and Pozuelo de Aragón Geyser Loop, a 22.9 miles (36.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores varied natural settings. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Borja – Tarazona loop from Antigua Estacion de Ainzon, a difficult 43.9 miles (70.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the region's mountainous backdrop. Local touring cyclists also love the Viewpoint – Bike shop  loop from Santuario de Misericordia, a 49.1 miles (79.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Borja is defined by expansive forests, mountainous terrain, and scenic routes leading to historical sites like the Liplje Monastery. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Borja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from 9 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Borja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Borja?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Borja, offering a variety of experiences through its expansive forests and mountainous terrain. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes around Borja?
A: Borja offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes. You'll find 1 easy route, 9 moderate routes, and 15 difficult routes, ensuring options for cyclists of all experience levels. The difficult routes often feature significant elevation gain through the region's mountainous backdrop.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Borja?
A: While many routes in Borja are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. For families, we recommend looking for routes with minimal elevation changes and shorter distances. Always check the route details on komoot for specific suitability.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on touring cycling routes in Borja?
A: Touring cycling in Borja is characterized by its abundant coniferous and deciduous forests, which cover the slopes of its mountainous terrain. You'll cycle through picturesque forests, with routes offering scenic views and the chance to explore natural beauty. The region is known for its 'air spa' quality due to the extensive pine forests.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Borja?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Borja are designed as loops. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993120360/burosque-enchanted-forest-and-pozuelo-de-aragon-geyser-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burosque Enchanted Forest and Pozuelo de Aragón Geyser Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 36.9 km route, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borja – Tarazona loop from Antigua Estacion de Ainzon&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 70.8 km.

Q: What are some scenic stops or points of interest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty of the forests and mountains, touring cycling routes in Borja can lead you to interesting landmarks. You might pass through the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1755663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazona&lt;/a&gt;, or near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1812727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarazonica Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Borja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Borja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the forests, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Borja offers several challenging long-distance routes for experienced touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31885295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint – Bike shop 🚲 loop from Santuario de Misericordia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 79 km trail, often taking over 7 hours to complete. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borja – Castle of Talamantes loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 56.7 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Borja?
A: Given Borja's mountainous terrain and extensive forests, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder weather to the higher elevations.

Q: Can I find routes that explore historical sites in Borja?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. The region research mentions the 13th-century Liplje Monastery at the foothills of Borja, which can be an attractive destination. While not directly linked in the provided routes, the journey to such sites often passes through scenic forests, making for an appealing ride. You can also find routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the town of Borja itself?
A: Yes, some routes originate from or pass through the town of Borja. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31882082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borja – Chapel of the Estanca de Borja loop from Borja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 55.9 km route that starts and ends in Borja, allowing you to explore the surrounding area while staying connected to the town.
tips: We are in the town of Bureta, a small and quiet town where we will find the Enchanted Burosque, a park decorated with miniature wooden models made with great ingenuity. And in the urban nucleus we will also see that in almost all the houses there are little houses or some figure and other small details that have given a new life to the town. If we choose this town to stop to rest, we can have a coffee in the courtyard of the Castle.
The geyser spouts over 2 meters high. Analysis has revealed that the hot springs have mineral healing properties.

Borja has a pronounced continental climate with limited precipitation of 350 to 450 mm per year. Hail and frost are common in spring and a cold, dry northerly wind (the cierzo) is typical of the area.

faqs.question: Where does this circular cycling route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts and ends in the historic town of Borja. You should be able to find parking within the town, which serves as an excellent base for exploring the area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved, making it well-suited for touring cycling. While mostly smooth, you will encounter segments of narrow paths, streets, and a short stretch on a national road. It's a moderate route, so be prepared for some varied surfaces.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 36.9 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 22 minutes to complete by bike, assuming a moderate pace.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the whimsical Burosque Encantado (Enchanted Forest) in Bureta, known for its miniature wooden models, and the Pozuelo de Aragón Geyser. The route also offers picturesque paths surrounded by trees and views of the rural landscapes of Campo de Borja.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this route requires a good physical condition. While largely paved, its length and elevation gain of about 320 meters mean it's best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the continental climate with limited precipitation in the Campo de Borja region, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for cycling. The weather is typically milder, offering ideal conditions to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Enchanted Forest of Bureta and Pozuelo de Aragon Geyser circular from Borja trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially on shared paths and near roads. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. You can freely enjoy the public paths and roads.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Vía Verde del Campo de Borja, Camino Jacobeo Castellano-Aragonés: Gallur-Borja, Camino Ambel a Fuendejalón, Calle Tajada, and Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the rural and agricultural landscapes of the Campo de Borja district, known for its winegrowing heritage. Expect picturesque paths surrounded by trees, charming village views, and the unique artistic installations of the Enchanted Forest.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Borja, a historic town where you can find various amenities, including cafes, pubs, and restaurants. It's a good idea to carry snacks and water for the cycling portion itself, as options might be limited between towns.
